mechanism_narrative: A sustained low level of the federal funds rate indicates accommodative monetary policy, which reduces the cost of capital and encourages investment, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:low interest rates generally support equity valuations
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative or flat (median return < 0.01%) when FEDFUNDS is below 2% for more than 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market and potential economic contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as corporate earnings expectations decline.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE rises by >0.5pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales and housing starts indicates a broad-based weakening of consumer demand and investment, which negatively impacts corporate revenues and profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth rate < 0% AND HOUST 3-month growth rate < 0%
evidence_refs: fact:consumer spending and housing are key economic drivers
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of periods where both RSAFS and HOUST show negative 3-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high inflation, a sustained increase in the Consumer Price Index signals persistent inflationary pressures, which prompts tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:high inflation often leads to rate hikes
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when CPIAUCSL 12-month growth exceeds 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Robust growth in real GDP indicates a strong and expanding economy, which translates into higher corporate earnings and investor confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP growth is a fundamental driver of equity markets
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of periods when GDPC1 shows positive quarter-over-quarter growth.

mechanism_narrative: A significant deceleration in non-farm payroll employment growth suggests a weakening labor market and potential economic slowdown, which reduces consumer spending power and corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth rate falls below 50,000 jobs per month
evidence_refs: fact:job growth is a key indicator of economic health
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when PAYEMS 3-month average growth falls below 50,000.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production reflects healthy manufacturing and mining sectors, indicating strong supply-side capacity and demand for goods, which supports corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:industrial production reflects real economic activity
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of periods when INDPRO shows positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is high, a concurrent decline in retail sales indicates that tight monetary policy is effectively curbing consumer demand, signaling a potential economic contraction and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of instances where FEDFUNDS is above 4.0% and RSAFS shows negative 3-month growth.
